 {"id":"bd-0e02","title":"Code smell: Inconsistent error handling patterns","description":"Commands use a mix of error handling patterns:
- FatalError()
- FatalErrorRespectJSON()
- fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, ...) + os.Exit(1)
- Silent continue
- Inconsistent resource cleanup (result.Close())

**Example in show.go lines 89-146:**
Different error paths use different patterns, some close resources, some don't.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Document error handling contract
- [ ] Standardize on consistent pattern
- [ ] Use defer for resource cleanup
- [ ] Create helper for common fetch-and-handle pattern
- [ ] Tests pass","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-28T18:59:30.118-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2025-12-30T15:44:43.362524-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T07:02:03.530317-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented: Added LogError, LogErrorRespectJSON, LogNotFound, LogNotFoundRespectJSON helpers to errors.go. Standardized show.go to use these helpers. Updated ERROR_HANDLING.md documentation. Tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-0e02","depends_on_id":"bd-ox1o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:00:23.683836-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-0fvq","title":"bd doctor should recommend bd prime migration for existing repos","description":"bd doctor should detect old beads integration patterns and recommend migrating to bd prime approach.

## Current behavior
- bd doctor checks if Claude hooks are installed globally
- Doesn't check project-level integration (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md)
- Doesn't recommend migration for repos using old patterns

## Desired behavior
bd doctor should detect and suggest:

1. **Old slash command pattern detected**
   - Check for /beads:* references in AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md
   - Suggest: These slash commands are deprecated, use bd prime hooks instead
   
2. **No agent documentation**
   - Check if AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md exists
   - Suggest: Run 'bd onboard' or 'bd setup claude' to document workflow
   
3. **Old MCP-only pattern**
   - Check for instructions to use MCP tools but no bd prime hooks
   - Suggest: Add bd prime hooks for better token efficiency

4. **Migration path**
   - Show: 'Run bd setup claude to add SessionStart/PreCompact hooks'
   - Show: 'Update AGENTS.md to reference bd prime instead of slash commands'

## Example output

⚠ Warning: Old beads integration detected in CLAUDE.md
  Found: /beads:* slash command references (deprecated)
  Recommend: Migrate to bd prime hooks for better token efficiency
  Fix: Run 'bd setup claude' and update CLAUDE.md

💡 Tip: bd prime + hooks reduces token usage by 80-99% vs slash commands
  MCP mode: ~50 tokens vs ~10.5k for full MCP scan
  CLI mode: ~1-2k tokens with automatic context recovery

## Benefits
- Helps existing repos adopt new best practices
- Clear migration path for users
- Better token efficiency messaging","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-12T03:20:25.567748-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T22:33:23.931274-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T22:33:23.931274-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-396j","title":"GetBlockedIssues shows external deps as blocking even when satisfied","description":"GetBlockedIssues (ready.go:385-493) shows external:* refs in the blocked_by list but doesn't check if they're actually satisfied using CheckExternalDep.

This can be confusing - an issue shows as blocked by external:project:capability even if that capability has been shipped (closed issue with provides: label exists).

Options:
1. Call CheckExternalDep for each external ref and filter satisfied ones from blocked_by
2. Add a note in output indicating external deps need lazy resolution
3. Document this is expected behavior (bd blocked shows all deps, bd ready shows resolved state)

Related: GetReadyWork correctly filters by external deps, but GetBlockedIssues doesn't.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:05.286304-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T21:48:38.086451-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T21:48:38.086451-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-396j","depends_on_id":"bd-zmmy","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:05.286971-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bhl","title":"Add sync rollback on git commit failure","description":"Part of GH#885 fix: graceful failure handling.

When git commit fails after export:
1. Detect the failure
2. Roll back SQLite metadata to pre-export state
3. Optionally restore JSONL from git HEAD
4. Report clear error with recovery instructions

This makes sync failures recoverable without manual intervention.

Depends on the core atomicity fix - this is defense in depth.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T13:53:27.971284-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2026-01-04T23:07:38.536388-08:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T23:07:38.536388-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented sync rollback on git commit failure (GH#885/bd-3bhl): 
- Added rollbackJSONLFromGit() helper in sync_git.go
- Changed exportToJSONL() to exportToJSONLDeferred() for atomic sync
- Added pendingExportResult tracking variable
- Call finalizeExport() only after successful git commit
- Rollback JSONL from git HEAD on commit failure with clear error message
- Coverage: regular commit, sync branch commit, external beads repo commit paths","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bhl","depends_on_id":"bd-pnx2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-04T13:53:55.869715-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma"},{"issue_id":"bd-3bhl","depends_on_id":"bd-usvv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T13:53:57.388449-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bsz","title":"gt mail send: support reading message body from stdin","description":"Currently gt mail send -m requires the message as a command-line argument, which causes shell escaping issues with backticks, quotes, and special characters.

Add support for reading message body from stdin:
- gt mail send addr -s 'Subject' --stdin  # Read body from stdin
- echo 'body' | gt mail send addr -s 'Subject' -m -  # Convention: -m - means stdin

This would allow:
cat <<'EOF' | gt mail send addr -s 'Subject' --stdin
Message with `backticks` and 'quotes' safely
EOF

Without this, agents struggle to send handoff messages containing code snippets.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-23T03:21:39.496208-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:44.443554-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:44.443554-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-3jrb","title":"bd close lacks cross-rig routing support","description":"The `bd close` command doesn't support cross-rig routing like `bd show` does.

**Reproduction:**
```
# From gastown directory
bd show bd-xyz    # Works (routes to beads)
bd close bd-xyz   # Fails with 'no issue found matching'
```

**Root cause:**
- `show.go` uses `needsRouting()` and `resolveAndGetIssueWithRouting()`
- `close.go` only uses `utils.ResolvePartialIDs()` without routing

**Other affected commands (likely):**
- reopen
- dep  
- comments
- label
- duplicate
- defer/undefer

**Fix:**
Add routing support to `close.go` similar to `show.go` lines 44-147.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-01T11:20:41.195141-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/gus","updated_at":"2026-01-01T11:24:25.322979-08:00","closed_at":"2026-01-01T11:24:25.322979-08:00","close_reason":"test"}

 {"id":"bd-70c4","title":"Gate await fields cleared by --no-daemon CLI access (not multi-repo)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-25T23:30:38.648182-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-26T23:38:47.972075-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-26T23:38:47.972075-08:00","comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-70c4","author":"mayor","text":"## Summary
Gate await fields (await_type, await_id, timeout_ns, waiters) are cleared when a CLI command accesses the database directly (--no-daemon) while the daemon is running. This is separate from the multi-repo issue fixed in bd-gr4q.

## Reproduction
1. Start daemon: bd daemon --start
2. Create gate: bd gate create --await timer:5s (fields stored correctly)
3. Verify: sqlite3 .beads/beads.db shows timer|5s
4. Run CLI with --no-daemon: bd show <id> --no-daemon --no-auto-import --no-auto-flush
5. Check again: fields are now empty

## Investigation Notes
- NOT caused by autoImportIfNewer (verified with --no-auto-import flag)
- NOT caused by HydrateFromMultiRepo (no multi-repo config, returns early)
- NOT caused by molecule loader (only creates new issues)
- NOT caused by migrations (gate_columns only adds columns)
- No database triggers found

The clearing happens somewhere in sqlite.NewWithTimeout() initialization or command execution path.

## Related
- bd-gr4q fixed the multi-repo path but this is a different code path
- The fix pattern (COALESCE/NULLIF) may need to be applied elsewhere","created_at":"2025-12-26T07:30:49Z"}]}

Should use utils.ResolvePartialID for proper partial ID resolution in direct mode (non-daemon).","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:22:57.586917-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}
 {"id":"bd-74w1","title":"Consolidate duplicate path-finding utilities (findJSONLPath, findBeadsDir, findGitRoot)","description":"Code health review found these functions defined in multiple places:

- findJSONLPath() in autoflush.go:45-73 and doctor/fix/migrate.go
- findBeadsDir() in autoimport.go:197-239 (with git worktree handling)
- findGitRoot() in autoimport.go:242-269 (Windows path conversion)

The beads package has public FindBeadsDir() and FindJSONLPath() APIs that should be used consistently.

Impact: Bug fixes need to be applied in multiple places. Git worktree handling may not be replicated everywhere.

Fix: Consolidate all implementations to use the beads package APIs. Remove duplicates.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:17:16.694293-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T21:13:46.83103-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T21:13:46.83103-08:00"}
